title: oxalis subsessilis
intro: Oxalis subsessilis, also known as the Sourgrass or Wood Sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. It is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds like oxalic acid and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as diarrhea, dysentery, and skin irritations, offering health benefits like anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Oxalis subsessilis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
